title: error loading cached network config: network "podman" not found in CNI cache
body:
### Issue Description

Yesterday I updated my server and rebooted it. It's the first time that the container "Nginx" stopped. Today I found out that nginx is not reachable since it's always getting an ip address from 10.88.0.x provided by interface "cni-podman0". So I tried adding --ip=192.168..188.73 to force it to use my own local network but it always giving me these errors:

ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config: network "podman" not found in CNI cache

WARN[0000] falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk

Error: error configuring network namespace for container fd3e72d5501b7501e39302013e97b66aa053d336f1316dfb3814830fc9aefb84: error adding pod nginx_nginx to CNI network "podman": failed to allocate all requested IPs: 192.168.188.73

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. pull image from docker.io
2. Run with sudo the next command
3. podman run -d --name nginx --restart unless-stopped --ip=192.168.188.73 -p 80:80 -p 81:81 -p 443:443 -v /home/lab-dell/nginx/data:/data -v /home/lab-dell/nginx/letsencrypt:/etc/letsencrypt jc21/nginx-proxy-manager:github-pr-2971 

### Describe the results you received

Errors when trying to force it to the correct ip address. If ran without "--ip" it gets an incorrect ip.



### Describe the results you expected

Nginx working with ip address 192.168.188.73 (enp1s0 interface)

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
v3.4.4 is super old, we cannot provide support such old versions upstream.

Regardless, this just cannot work. The default network uses a bridge+veth pair setup with ip range 10.88.0.0/16 and NAt's all external connections so this should just work. You cannot assign your host ip to a container, that would mean the same ip is used twice which will never work.

If you want to use the host network use --network host then port forwarding is not required/used.
